current_intent.target:
Yes. Your correction is important: **beauty that improves readability, calm, motivation, and orientation is part of utility**, not opposed to it. [nngroup](https://www.nngroup.com/articles/aesthetic-usability-effect/)

## Minimal patch set

Here’s a compact patch set that makes the page feel more refined, calmer, and more desirable **without turning it into a marketing page** and without changing the information model you already have in `index.html`. [ppl-ai-file-upload.s3.amazonaws](https://ppl-ai-file-upload.s3.amazonaws.com/web/direct-files/attachments/149814877/39595d22-e475-4527-8d35-1210cdd5fd55/index.html)

### Typography
- Use a cleaner UI/body font plus a serious monospace for structural content. A pairing like **Inter or Geist for prose** and **JetBrains Mono or Fira Code for code/paths/gates** improves readability and gives the page a more intentional, premium feel. [uxpin](https://www.uxpin.com/studio/blog/ultimate-guide-to-typography-accessibility-testing/)
- Tighten the type system to 4 levels max: page title, section title, body, metadata. This reduces visual noise and makes the page feel calmer. [accessibility.huit.harvard](https://accessibility.huit.harvard.edu/design-readability)
- Keep all paths, filenames, conditions, and outputs in monospace so the “mechanical” parts of the workflow stand out instantly. [ppl-ai-file-upload.s3.amazonaws](https://ppl-ai-file-upload.s3.amazonaws.com/web/direct-files/attachments/149814877/39595d22-e475-4527-8d35-1210cdd5fd55/index.html)

### Color and surfaces
- Keep the existing semantic colors for steps, gates, terminals, and route-outs, but soften the surrounding surfaces so the colors feel more deliberate and less harsh. [ppl-ai-file-upload.s3.amazonaws](https://ppl-ai-file-upload.s3.amazonaws.com/web/direct-files/attachments/149814877/39595d22-e475-4527-8d35-1210cdd5fd55/index.html)
- Use layered neutral backgrounds, subtle borders, and one restrained accent color for interactive states. This creates that “sexy but serious” feeling without looking like a landing page. [learn.microsoft](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-platform/well-architected/experience-optimization/visual-design)
- Add a faint highlighted background when a section is targeted via hash. That makes direct linking feel polished and useful. [developer.mescius](https://developer.mescius.com/blogs/how-to-use-the-aesthetic-usability-effect-in-javascript-application)

### Readability rhythm
- Increase line-height slightly for prose, but reduce wasted vertical gaps between related elements. The goal is “dense but breathable,” not “airy”. [designsystem.digital](https://designsystem.digital.gov/components/typography/)
- Break long step blocks with lightweight labels such as **Inputs**, **Outputs**, **Gate**, using quiet pill styling rather than heavy boxes. That improves scan speed across your workflow sections. [ppl-ai-file-upload.s3.amazonaws](https://ppl-ai-file-upload.s3.amazonaws.com/web/direct-files/attachments/149814877/39595d22-e475-4527-8d35-1210cdd5fd55/index.html)
- Give artifacts and proof/provenance sections a slightly different surface treatment so the eye recognizes them as “outputs,” not just more prose. [ppl-ai-file-upload.s3.amazonaws](https://ppl-ai-file-upload.s3.amazonaws.com/web/direct-files/attachments/149814877/39595d22-e475-4527-8d35-1210cdd5fd55/index.html)

### Interaction polish
- Add **copy buttons** to every code fence, path, and artifact path. This is one of the highest-value improvements for a terminal-oriented user. [archbee](https://www.archbee.com/blog/technical-documentation-usability)
- Add stable anchors for steps, gates, route-outs, terminals, and artifacts, and visually flash the target section when linked. That makes the page feel responsive and shareable. [developer.mescius](https://developer.mescius.com/blogs/how-to-use-the-aesthetic-usability-effect-in-javascript-application)
- Use subtle hover and focus transitions on buttons, TOC entries, and accordion headers. Small motion increases perceived quality without becoming decorative. [learn.microsoft](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-platform/well-architected/experience-optimization/visual-design)

### What to style like a tool
- Keep the **proof/artifact outputs** visually crisp and monospace-heavy, but don’t overdo terminal chrome. A restrained “console card” treatment is enough: darker inset surface, stronger border, tabular numbers, compact spacing. [ppl-ai-file-upload.s3.amazonaws](https://ppl-ai-file-upload.s3.amazonaws.com/web/direct-files/attachments/149814877/39595d22-e475-4527-8d35-1210cdd5fd55/index.html)
- Make **gate badges** more legible with stronger contrast and a slightly bolder weight. They are decision points, so they should pop functionally and aesthetically. [ppl-ai-file-upload.s3.amazonaws](https://ppl-ai-file-upload.s3.amazonaws.com/web/direct-files/attachments/149814877/39595d22-e475-4527-8d35-1210cdd5fd55/index.html)
